title: Bench with Shelf and Scrap Wood Top
summary: A small end-of-bed bench with jigsaw-cut leg profiles, a lower shelf, and a slatted top made from scrap 1x4. A quick offcut-eater.
description:
This is the perfect project for the pile of 1x4 offcuts every shop accumulates — the seat is nine short slats, so odd lengths become a feature rather than waste. It's sized to sit at the end of a bed or in an entryway: a two-sided base with shaped 1x12 legs, a lower shelf, and a slatted top.

The only shaped work is cutting the leg profile out of the 1x12s with a jigsaw. Cut both identical — trace one onto the other or use the first as a template — so the bench sits level. Everything else is butt joints held with pocket screws or self-tapping screws.

The angled side aprons (45° ends, not parallel) tie the two leg panels together and set the seat width. Build the base square, then nail the slats down across the top with a small consistent gap. Because it's simple and cheap, it's a great candidate for a bolder paint or a mix of stained scrap slats for a reclaimed look.

tools.note: Cutting the shaped leg profile out of the 1x12s.

materials.name: 1x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Side aprons and top slats — scrap 1x4 works great for the slats.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping screws, 2"

cutList.note: Cut the shaped leg profile with a jigsaw; make both identical.
Both ends cut at 45° off square, not parallel.

steps.title: Cut the legs
steps.body: Cut the shaped leg profile out of the 1x12s with a jigsaw as shown in the diagram. Cut both identical — trace the first onto the second — so the bench sits level.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the bottom shelf between the two shaped legs to join them into a base. Keep it square.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping screws, 2"

steps.title: Attach the side aprons
steps.body: Attach the side aprons (45° ends, not parallel) to the leg panels. These tie the base together and set the seat width.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping screws, 2"

steps.title: Attach the seat slats and finish
steps.body: Nail the nine top slats across the aprons with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, spacing them with a small consistent gap. Mixing lengths of scrap is fine — the slats hide the joints. Fill, sand, and finish.

images.alt: A small bench with a slatted top, a lower shelf, and shaped legs
